description: Evaluates the end-to-end solution architecture skill across different system types, integration patterns, and security models. Each eval targets a specific architectural capability.

evals.name: full-7-section-microservices
evals.prompt: Design the full solution architecture for an e-commerce platform with 5 microservices, mobile and web channels, and third-party payment integration.
evals.expected_output: Complete 7-section architecture: solution view (C4 containers), integration architecture, channel/BFF design, identity/security (Zero Trust), observability (SLI/SLO), cross-cutting concerns, and transition plan.
expectations: C4 container diagram identifies all 5 microservices plus payment gateway as external system
Integration architecture specifies sync vs async patterns per service boundary
Channel architecture includes BFF for mobile and web with justification
Identity section covers OAuth2/OIDC for customer auth and PCI-DSS for payment
Observability defines SLIs and SLOs for critical paths (checkout, payment)
Cross-cutting concerns include circuit breaker for payment gateway dependency
Transition plan uses strangler fig or phased migration with rollback criteria

evals.name: legacy-mainframe-integration
evals.prompt: Plan the API gateway strategy to integrate our COBOL mainframe with a new React frontend and mobile app.
evals.expected_output: Architecture handles legacy mainframe edge case: impedance mismatch addressed, integration layer with adapter/translator, strangler fig pattern, eventual retirement timeline.
expectations: Identifies impedance mismatch: batch vs real-time, EBCDIC vs UTF-8
Proposes integration layer with adapter pattern between mainframe and API gateway
API gateway handles routing, rate limiting, and request transformation
BFF pattern recommended for divergent mobile vs web needs
Strangler fig migration strategy with parallel running phase
Data consistency model addresses eventual consistency during transition

evals.name: zero-trust-security-focus
evals.prompt: Define the identity and security architecture for a healthcare platform handling PHI data with HIPAA compliance.
evals.expected_output: Deep S4 (Identity & Security) with HIPAA compliance mapping, Zero Trust throughout, encryption in transit and at rest, audit trails, data residency, consent management.
expectations: Zero Trust applied: every request authenticated and authorized
HIPAA compliance mapped to specific architectural decisions
Encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest explicitly specified
PII/PHI tokenization or masking strategy defined
Audit trails for all data access with retention policy
MFA required for all staff access to PHI systems
Data classification includes restricted tier for PHI

evals.name: observability-stack-design
evals.prompt: Set up observability for our distributed order management system with 12 services across 3 regions.
evals.expected_output: Deep S5 (Observability) with structured logging, distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry), metrics at application/infra/business levels, alerting, and SLI/SLO definitions.
expectations: Structured logging with correlation IDs across all 12 services
Distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry and sampling strategy for 3 regions
Metrics at three levels: application (RPS, latency p50/p95/p99), infrastructure, business
SLIs defined for critical order flow paths
SLOs with targets and SLA consequence mapping
Alerting with escalation paths and alert fatigue management
Dashboards designed for executive, operational, and debugging audiences

evals.name: executive-variant-abbreviated
evals.prompt: Design the solution architecture for our CRM modernization. Use variante ejecutiva.
evals.expected_output: Abbreviated output (~40%) covering only S1 (solution view), S2 (integration), and S4 (security). Omits channels, observability, cross-cutting, and transition.
expectations: Output contains only S1, S2, and S4 sections
S1 includes C4 container diagram with system boundaries
S2 covers integration patterns with trade-off justification
S4 covers authentication, authorization, and encryption
Does not include S3 (channels), S5 (observability), S6 (cross-cutting), or S7 (transition)
